title: scurrula venosa
intro: Scurrula venosa, also known as the poison tree or poison vine, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its woody climber with milky sap and small, white flowers. Traditionally, Scurrula venosa has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The plant's extracts have been shown to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Scurrula venosa is often used in combination with other plants to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
